procedural_history: Williamson v Williamson 2016 BCCA 87 reaches the British Columbia Court of Appeal on appeal from a BC Supreme Court family-law decision. The case engages questions of custody modification and the deployment of structured reunification-programme intervention in the context of sustained parental-alienation-adjacent fact-patterns under the BC Family Law Act 2011 + federal Divorce Act framework. The decision is the canonical Canadian citation for residential-reunification-programme litigation in Canadian family-court jurisprudence — particularly engaging Family Reflections Reunification Program (Kathleen Reay, BC) as the only Canadian-based residential reunification programme regularly named in Canadian custody judgments.
summary: Williamson v Williamson 2016 BCCA 87 is the canonical Canadian appellate citation for residential-reunification-programme intervention in PA-adjacent custody-modification fact-patterns. The decision engages Family Reflections Reunification Program (Kathleen Reay, BC) — the only Canadian-based residential reunification programme regularly named in Canadian custody jurisprudence — alongside US extra-regulator programmes (Family Bridges Warshak + Overcoming Barriers AFCC). The decision operates at provincial-Court-of-Appeal level under BC Family Law Act 2011 + federal Divorce Act framework. Together with L.A.G. v. D.K.B. 2009 CanLII 6452 (ON SC) (canonical Ontario PA decision) Williamson v Williamson establishes the Canadian appellate jurisprudence on PA-adjacent custody-modification + reunification-programme intervention.
holding: Where sustained alienating conduct by one parent causes substantive harm to the child's relationship with the other non-custodial parent, the court may consider deploying structured reunification-programme intervention as part of custody-modification disposition under the BC Family Law Act 2011 + federal Divorce Act framework. The appellate court engages the substantive welfare considerations under provincial best-interests standard and federal s.16 best-interests considerations (pre-Bill C-78 reform; Bill C-78 in force March 2021 subsequently rewrote s.16). Family Reflections Reunification Program (Reay BC) is engaged as Canadian-based residential reunification provider — the decision establishes appellate-court engagement with reunification-programme disposition in Canadian family-law jurisprudence.
outcome: BC Court of Appeal disposition engaging structured reunification-programme intervention in PA-adjacent custody-modification context. Williamson v Williamson establishes Canadian appellate jurisprudence on reunification-programme deployment. Subsequent Canadian custody decisions cite Williamson as canonical authority for residential-reunification-programme intervention.

comparative_jurisprudence.caption: L.A.G. v. D.K.B., 2009 CanLII 6452 (ON SC)
comparative_jurisprudence.relevance: Canonical Ontario Superior Court of Justice PA decision predating Williamson. L.A.G. v. D.K.B. engages OCL s.112 CLRA Clinical Investigator framework; Williamson v Williamson engages BC reunification-programme intervention. Together the two decisions establish Canadian provincial jurisprudence at Ontario + BC levels on PA-adjacent custody disposition.

comparative_jurisprudence.caption: Re Y [2026] EWFC 38 (Sir Andrew McFarlane P, England and Wales)
comparative_jurisprudence.relevance: Comparative apex-appellate engagement. Re Y operates UK-wide evaluator-quality apex authority via HCPC-registration requirement; Williamson v Williamson operates BC appellate engagement with reunification-programme disposition. Both engage common-law family-court appellate jurisprudence on PA-adjacent intervention at provincial / unified level.

comparative_jurisprudence.caption: TEN v TEO and another appeal [2020] SGHCF 20 (Singapore)
comparative_jurisprudence.relevance: Comparative HC engagement with PA-adjacent fact-patterns. Debbie Ong J declined to transfer care after sustained alienation had hardened over years; Williamson v Williamson engages structured reunification-programme intervention. The two decisions illustrate the spectrum of dispositions available under common-law welfare framework — declined transfer (Singapore) vs reunification-programme intervention (Canada BC).

comparative_jurisprudence.caption: Green & Green [2024] FedCFamC1F 896 (Australia)
comparative_jurisprudence.relevance: Comparative Australian FCFCOA engagement post-2023 Schedule 2 Amendment Act. Green & Green operates substantive PA-engaging FCFCOA D1 disposition under Court Children's Report regime; Williamson v Williamson operates BC appellate reunification-programme engagement. Both engage common-law family-court structured intervention frameworks.

subsequent_reception.citation: Williamson v Williamson 2016 BCCA 87 — canonical Canadian reunification-programme jurisprudence
subsequent_reception.summary: Williamson v Williamson cited in subsequent Canadian custody jurisprudence as canonical appellate authority for residential-reunification-programme intervention. Family Reflections Reunification Program (Reay BC) engagement preserved as Canadian-based provider reference within broader US-Canadian reunification-programme landscape (Family Bridges Warshak + Overcoming Barriers AFCC US + Family Reflections Reay BC).

subsequent_reception.citation: Provincial-college regulator scrutiny of reunification-programme practitioners
subsequent_reception.summary: Provincial-college regulators (CPO Ontario + CPBA-BC + OPQ Quebec + CAP Alberta) operate jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction scrutiny of reunification-programme practitioners. Canadian provincial-college fragmentation produces no single national-level credentialing for reunification-programme deployment — context within which Williamson v Williamson operates.

sources.title: Family Law Act SBC 2011 c.25 (British Columbia)

editorial_notes: Williamson v Williamson 2016 BCCA 87 is the canonical Canadian appellate citation for residential-reunification-programme intervention in PA-adjacent custody-modification jurisprudence.
Decision engages Family Reflections Reunification Program (Kathleen Reay, BC) — the only Canadian-based residential reunification programme regularly named in Canadian custody jurisprudence.
Party identity protected per Canadian provincial-superior-court family-law convention (Williamson generic surname placeholder).
Sources: CanLII for case-law (2016 BCCA 87) + bclaws.gov.bc.ca for BC Family Law Act 2011 + laws-lois.justice.gc.ca for federal Divorce Act.
Cross-link to L.A.G. v. D.K.B. 2009 CanLII 6452 (ON SC) preserved as Ontario provincial-level companion canonical decision.

Cross-jurisdictional reunification-programme engagement (US Family Bridges + Overcoming Barriers + Canadian Family Reflections + UK Cafcass + AU FRW + NZ Goldson + SG CAPS + HK SWD Centres) documented in companion evidence page reunification-programmes-and-regulation.
